name: Cowboy Country
description_short: Cowboy Country Federal Credit Union serves the Premont, TX community with savings accounts, checking accounts, auto loans, and digital banking services for members.
description_long:
Cowboy Country Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving the Premont, Texas community and surrounding service areas. The credit union is federally chartered and regulated by the NCUA, providing traditional credit union services focused on personal and member financial well-being rather than profit maximization.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of deposit and lending products. On the deposit side, members can access regular share savings accounts (2,497 active accounts), share certificates/CDs (94 active accounts), and checking accounts called Share Draft accounts (855 active accounts with $2.67M in deposits). For borrowing, Cowboy Country provides auto financing with competitive rates—8.25% for new vehicles and 9.25% for used vehicles—serving over 300 new auto loan members and 200 used auto loan members with an average loan size of $29,000. The credit union also advertises mortgage options, investment planning, and wealth management services, though specific details on these products are limited in available documentation.

Cowboy Country distinguishes itself through member-centric credit union operations and modern digital capabilities. The institution offers mobile banking, e-statements, remote deposit capture, online bill pay, and 24/7 account access. These digital tools align the credit union with contemporary banking expectations while maintaining credit union principles of personalized member service. The credit union advertises competitive rates, low minimum balance requirements on CDs, and exclusive member benefits and discounts.

A significant limitation is the lack of detailed rate information and product specificity on the public-facing site. While auto loan rates are provided, savings and CD rates are not disclosed, requiring direct contact. The credit union's service area appears geographically limited to Premont and surrounding regions, which may restrict membership eligibility. Additionally, the website content suggests but does not confirm the availability of mortgage, investment, and retirement planning services—these may require member inquiry to verify current offerings and specific terms. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Share Savings Accounts (regular savings with competitive rates)
Share Draft Accounts (checking accounts with debit card and check access)
Mortgage Financing Options (mentioned but not detailed)
Mobile Banking App (check balances, transfer funds, pay bills)
Online Banking Portal (account access, transfers, bill pay)
Investment and Retirement Planning (mentioned but not detailed)
Wealth Management Services (mentioned but not detailed)
pros: Federally insured credit union with NCUA oversight protecting member deposits
Competitive auto loan rates: 8.25% for new vehicles, 9.25% for used vehicles
Modern digital banking including mobile app, remote deposit capture, and online bill pay
Low-minimum-balance share certificates (CDs) for flexible savings options
Member-owned structure aligns institutional incentives with member financial benefit rather than shareholder profit
Local community presence in Premont, TX with personalized member service
E-statements and paperless options supporting environmental sustainability
cons: Savings and CD rates not publicly disclosed—members must contact directly for rate comparison
Geographic service limitation to Premont, TX area may restrict membership eligibility
Mortgage, investment planning, and wealth management services mentioned but not detailed; availability unconfirmed
No information on loan approval criteria, processing times, or online application capability
Limited branch presence typical of smaller credit unions may reduce physical banking convenience
best_for: Premont, TX residents and employees of member-eligible organizations seeking community-based banking
Auto buyers wanting competitive rates with personalized local credit union service
Members prioritizing digital convenience alongside traditional personal banking relationships
Savers seeking NCUA-insured deposits with competitive rates on savings and certificate products
diagnosis: Cowboy Country Federal Credit Union is best for Premont-area residents and eligible members seeking straightforward banking with competitive auto loan rates and modern digital tools. The main caveat is that detailed product rates and terms require direct contact, and membership eligibility is geographically restricted to the Premont service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
